paper_title: Influence of Pre-Measurement HCl Rinse and Local Upwelling Model in Akasaka Limestone Study

methods.description: This method involves removing carbonate from limestone samples using HCl to prepare them for nitrogen isotope analysis. The purpose is to address the low total nitrogen (TN) and total organic carbon (TOC) contents in the rocks, which can affect the δ15N values.

steps.description: Remove carbonate in the analyzed limestone by reaction with HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: react limestone samples with HCl to remove carbonate

steps.description: Consider the potential δ15N changes of samples via HCl rinse as the potential uncertainty in the estimation of δD in the Capitanian ocean.
steps.searchable_summary: assess δ15N changes as uncertainty in δD estimation

methods.description: This method models the nitrogen isotope composition in the Akasaka limestone by simulating the upwelling of deep water along a seamount. It uses a five-box model to examine the δ15N value of particulate organic nitrogen (PON) in the surface ocean.

steps.description: Employ a five-box model to examine the δ15N value of PON in the surface ocean box U around the seamount top.
steps.searchable_summary: use five-box model to simulate δ15N of PON

steps.description: Calculate export production and concentrations of O2, nitrate, ammonium, phosphate, and H2S in the box UM based on steady-state mass balance equations.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate chemical concentrations using mass balance equations
outputs: concentrations of O2, nitrate, ammonium, phosphate, H2S

steps.description: Determine the upwelling rate of water mass from UM to U (RU) by the sum of advective flows A and B.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate upwelling rate RU from advective flows

steps.description: Examine the influence of global oceanic redox conditions on δPON, focusing on O2 concentrations of the outer open-ocean boxes I and D.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze impact of redox conditions on δPON
